commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The contributor needs to update the generated runtime and provide real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over an external PR that currently lacks sufficient real proof.; Needs attention: The security fix is incomplete because the gateway-served browser asset still contains the original iconMarkup innerHTML sink.; Review finding: [P1] Update the served viewer runtime — extensions/diffs/src/viewer-client.ts:... (sha=5091e2089d0535b66084cb663c66a40cccb72845)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the diffs viewer toolbar button API from raw SVG markup strings to typed icon names backed by a fixed SVG map.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main serves `extensions/diffs/assets/viewer-runtime.js`, and that asset still contains `innerHTML=e.iconMarkup`. I did not run a browser/gateway repro because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The contributor needs to update the generated runtime and provide real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over an external PR that currently lacks sufficient real proof.

target.title: fix(diffs): replace iconMarkup string with ToolbarIconName enum to el…
target.body: ## Summary - Replace `createToolbarButton`'s `iconMarkup: string` parameter with `icon: ToolbarIconName`, a union of 8 known icon name literals - Move SVG generation into a sealed `toolbarIconSvg` map so `innerHTML` only receives compile-time-known strings - Remove 5 old icon functions (`splitIcon`/`unifiedIcon`/`wrapIcon`/`backgroundIcon`/`themeIcon`); callers now pass icon name literals - Closes #83918 ## Verification ### Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: `button.innerHTML = params.iconMarkup` in `createToolbarButton` accepted arbitrary strings, making it an XSS sink if `iconMarkup` ever became caller-controlled (issue #83918). The fix restricts the parameter to a `ToolbarIconName` union type, eliminating the sink at the type level. Real environment tested: Linux, Node v22 Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash # Structural checks node -e " const fs = require('fs'); const src = fs.readFileSync('extensions/diffs/src/viewer-client.ts', 'utf8'); console.log('Has ToolbarIconName type:', src.includes('type ToolbarIconName')); console.log('Has toolbarIconSvg map:', src.includes('const toolbarIconSvg')); console.log('No iconMarkup: string:', !src.includes('iconMarkup: string')); console.log('innerHTML only via toolbarIconSvg:', src.includes('button.innerHTML = toolbarIconSvg[params.icon]')); console.log('No old icon functions:', !src.includes('function splitIcon(') && !src.includes('function wrapIcon(')); " # XSS baseline node -e " const { JSDOM } = require('jsdom'); const dom = new JSDOM(''); const btn = dom.window.document.createElement('button'); btn.innerHTML = ''; console.log('XSS baseline - onerror injected:', btn.innerHTML.includes('onerror')); " # Type check npx tsc --noEmit --project extensions/diffs/tsconfig.json ``` Evidence after fix: ``` # Structural checks Has ToolbarIconName type: true Has toolbarIconSvg map: true No iconMarkup: string: true innerHTML only via toolbarIconSvg: true No old icon functions: true # XSS baseline XSS baseline - onerror injected: true # Type check (no output — passed) ``` Observed result after fix: `createToolbarButton` now accepts only `ToolbarIconName` literals (`split` | `unified` | `wrap-on` | `wrap-off` | `background-on` | `background-off` | `theme-dark` | `theme-light`). SVG strings are sealed in `toolbarIconSvg` and never derived from external input. TypeScript rejects any arbitrary string at compile time. The single `innerHTML` assignment reads exclusively from the typed map. All 8 icon names resolve to safe SVG with no `onerror`/`
target.author: tanshanshan
